mission.title: knative-eventing: ApiServerSource should be able to watch resources in other namespaces besides the one in which it resides
mission.description: ApiServerSource should be able to watch resources in other namespaces besides the one in which it resides. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative-eventing deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative-eventing version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
helm list -n knative-eventing 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative-eventing installation.

steps.title: Review knative-eventing configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative-eventing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get configmap -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative-eventing
```
**Problem**
currently apiserversource can only watch resources within the namespace in which it was created.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ApiServerSource should be able to watch resources in other…
steps.description:
Partially fixes #4481 
Fixes points 1. and 2. from https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/6145#issuecomment-1039506502
The automated reporting of broken traces would be done in a separate PR.
- register Zipkin exporter using trace config from the config-tracing config map in Knative Eventing namespace
- adds trace instrumentation to these components: wathola-sender, wathola-forwarder, wathola-receiver
- if config-tracing enables tracing and there's a backend storage (Zipkin or Jaeger) it

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "ApiServerSource should be able to watch resources in other…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Partially fixes #4481 
Fixes points 1. and 2. from https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/6145#issuecomment-1039506502
The automated reporting of broken traces would be done in a separate PR.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative-eventing installed or the issue environment reproducible.
